title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: INLA — integrated nested Laplace approximation (Rue, Martino, & Chopin, 2009) — extraordinarily fast Bayesian backend. cost speed structural assumption: model must latent Gaussian model (LGM). assumption holds, INLA returns posterior marginals accurate several decimal places. hold, INLA still returns something — something approximation whose error hard bound easy miss. latent Gaussian model form yi∣𝛈,𝛉∼π(yi∣ηi,𝛉)𝛈=(η1,…,ηn)=𝐀𝐱,𝐱∣𝛉∼𝒩(𝟎,𝐐(𝛉)−1) \begin{aligned} y_i \mid \boldsymbol{\eta}, \boldsymbol{\theta}   &\sim \pi(y_i \mid \eta_i, \boldsymbol{\theta}) \\ \boldsymbol{\eta} = (\eta_1, \dots, \eta_n)   &= \mathbf{}\, \mathbf{x}, \\ \mathbf{x} \mid \boldsymbol{\theta}   &\sim \mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},\, \mathbf{Q}(\boldsymbol{\theta})^{-1}) \end{aligned} latent field 𝐱\mathbf{x} conditionally Gaussian given small (≤15\le 15, practice) hyperparameter vector 𝛉\boldsymbol{\theta}, linear predictor 𝛈\boldsymbol{\eta} linear combination 𝐱\mathbf{x}. likelihood π(yi∣ηi,𝛉)\pi(y_i \mid \eta_i, \boldsymbol{\theta}) may non-Gaussian (Poisson, binomial, gamma, …) yiy_i depends 𝐱\mathbf{x} ηi\eta_i. Models violate LGM structure include: finite mixtures (multiple latent classes); distributional regression dispersion shape parameter; non-linear predictors; random-effect priors Gaussian (horseshoe, Cauchy, Student-tt latent field ). lgm_gate() structural-first filter decides, calling INLA, whether model LGM class. runs six checks plus seventh post-fit numerical check. Refusals non-silent construction: every refusal carries rule fired, one-line gloss, re-route suggestion (typically: use greta backend), documented override path.

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: Checks 1–6 fire structurally (without running INLA) fb_terms intermediate representation (IR). Check 7 lives inside emit_inla() non-overridable: numerically failed fit discarded, never quietly returned.

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: simplest LGM-feasible model Gaussian random intercept small dataset. use lme4::sleepstudy. fb_from_brms() builds IR; lgm_gate() runs six structural checks either returns augmented IR (lgm_compatible capability) structured refusal. IR carries lgm_compatible. Internally, fb_inla() (INLA engine pin, sugar flexybayes(..., backend = "inla")) uses flag decide whether call emit_inla() (), refuse structured error (). fit$num_check carries verdict check 7: mode_status, mlik_finite, boundary_proximity. three pass well-conditioned Gaussian random-intercept fit.
code: library(flexyBayes) data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4") fb_t <- flexyBayes:::fb_from_brms(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject),   data = sleepstudy ) res <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(fb_t) class(res) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list" res$capabilities #> [1] "lgm_compatible"      "gretaR_slot_dormant" fit <- fb_inla(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,                verbose = FALSE) fit$num_check       # post-fit check 7 #> $pass #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $reasons #> character(0)

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: now demonstrate four representative refusals. one resolves structured lgm_refusal object whose print method names rule, gloss, re-route, override path.

what: 4.1 Refusal pattern A — random slopes (caught by fb_from_brms, before the gate)
title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: brms-formula random slope rejected parse time, fb_terms IR ever built. error message names limitation directly: asreml-format entry flexybayes(fixed = ..., random = ...) accepts random-slope-equivalent constructions routes greta — see hierarchical models asreml-shaped formulas vignettes.
code: fb_brms(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), data = sleepstudy) #> Error: #> ! Correlated random slopes (x | g) are not yet supported. #> Uncorrelated random slopes (x || g) are supported -- they fit the #> marginal slope and intercept variances independently and are equivalent #> to the correlated form when the correlation is small. #> #> If your model needs the correlation parameter, defer to a #> future release (structured-covariance representation). #>  #> Workaround: re-fit as (x || g) if the correlation is not of inferential #> interest, or use backend = "greta" via fb_brms() with a hand-rolled #> covariance prior. #>  #> Got: (Days | Subject)

what: 4.2 Refusal pattern B — family outside the INLA roster (check 1)
title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: INLA carries internal roster supported likelihoods (around 100 families INLA::inla.models() queried live). family roster Laplace-approximated INLA. construct fictitious family name make refusal visible: structured refusal names rule fired (family_allowlist), diagnostic (fb$family = "imaginary_invented_family"), suggested re-route (fb_brms() Stan passthrough; fb_greta() – equivalently flexybayes(..., backend = "greta") – broader flexyBayes formula path), override path. Note package never silently approximates: user always sees INLA refused.
code: fb_t_bad <- fb_t fb_t_bad$family <- "imaginary_invented_family" ref <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(fb_t_bad) class(ref) #> [1] "lgm_refusal" "list" ref #> flexyBayes: INLA backend refused for this model. #> Reasons (1 structural failure): #>   - [family_allowlist] family "imaginary_invented_family" is not in the INLA likelihood allowlist (no built-in Laplace machinery). #>     Diagnostic: fb$family = "imaginary_invented_family" #> Re-route: try backend = "brms" via fb_brms() for the Stan passthrough #>   (model must be in the brms corpus), or backend = "greta" for the broader #>   flexyBayes formula path. NIMBLE is planned future work, not yet implemented. #> For mixtures with <= 2 components, the planned INLA-within-MCMC escape #>   (Gomez-Rubio & Rue 2018) is on the roadmap. #> Override (not recommended): lgm_gate(fb, force = "inla", #>   acknowledge_silent_bias_risk = TRUE, reason = "<your reason>") #> Docs: vignette("flexyBayes-11-lgm-feasibility").

what: 4.3 Refusal pattern C — non-Gaussian RE prior (check 4)
title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: latent field LGM Gaussian construction. horseshoe, Cauchy, Student-tt prior random-effect field (random-effect standard deviation, always hyperparameter) violates assumption. fb_prior() DSL expose target latent field directly, refusal constructed setting explicit non-Gaussian RE-prior entry IR: Real horseshoe--latent-field shrinkage best handled greta backend; future release add horseshoe target DSL greta side.
code: fb_t_horseshoe <- fb_t fb_t_horseshoe$priors <- structure(   list(re = list(list(family = "horseshoe", target = "u_Subject"))),   class = "fb_prior" ) ref_h <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(fb_t_horseshoe) ref_h #> flexyBayes: INLA backend refused for this model. #> Reasons (1 structural failure): #>   - [re_gaussian_prior] non-Gaussian random-effect prior detected (horseshoe). INLA's latent field is Gaussian by construction. #>     Diagnostic: fb$priors$re entries with non-Gaussian family #> Re-route: try backend = "brms" via fb_brms() for the Stan passthrough #>   (model must be in the brms corpus), or backend = "greta" for the broader #>   flexyBayes formula path. NIMBLE is planned future work, not yet implemented. #> For mixtures with <= 2 components, the planned INLA-within-MCMC escape #>   (Gomez-Rubio & Rue 2018) is on the roadmap. #> Override (not recommended): lgm_gate(fb, force = "inla", #>   acknowledge_silent_bias_risk = TRUE, reason = "<your reason>") #> Docs: vignette("flexyBayes-11-lgm-feasibility").

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: INLA integrates hyperparameter vector 𝛉\boldsymbol{\theta} numerical grid (central-composite design). grid scales exponentially dim⁡(𝛉)\dim(\boldsymbol{\theta}). INLA’s authors recommend dim⁡(𝛉)≤15\dim(\boldsymbol{\theta}) \le 15 tractable Laplace approximation. unstructured (us) genotype--environment covariance matrix across nine environments 9⋅10/2=459 \cdot 10 / 2 = 45 free entries — well budget. refusal cites hyperparam_budget reports count. re-route unambiguous: use greta backend (handles unstructured covariances natively via Cholesky parameterisation), reduce factor-analytic approximation (fa(env, k):gen) small k — latter standard agricultural-statistics move nenvn_{\text{env}} exceeds ten.
code: data(yan.winterwheat, package = "agridat") fb_us <- flexyBayes:::fb_from_asreml(   fixed = yield ~ env, random = ~ us(env):gen, rcov = NULL,   data = yan.winterwheat ) ref_us <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(fb_us) ref_us #> flexyBayes: INLA backend refused for this model. #> Reasons (2 structural failures): #>   - [hyperparam_budget] hyperparameter count = 46 exceeds hard limit 15 (CCD/grid integration intractable; INLA Laplace approximation deteriorates). #>     Diagnostic: count = likelihood (1) + random + rcov contributions #>   - [random_term_type_inla] random term type "us_gxe" (unstructured G×E) is outside the INLA emit allowlist. INLA's f() machinery does not currently represent this structured-covariance class without an SPDE / kronecker expansion that the current INLA emit does not produce. Re-route via backend = "greta". #>     Diagnostic: fb$random_terms type(s): us_gxe #> Re-route: try backend = "brms" via fb_brms() for the Stan passthrough #>   (model must be in the brms corpus), or backend = "greta" for the broader #>   flexyBayes formula path. NIMBLE is planned future work, not yet implemented. #> For mixtures with <= 2 components, the planned INLA-within-MCMC escape #>   (Gomez-Rubio & Rue 2018) is on the roadmap. #> Override (not recommended): lgm_gate(fb, force = "inla", #>   acknowledge_silent_bias_risk = TRUE, reason = "<your reason>") #> Docs: vignette("flexyBayes-11-lgm-feasibility").

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: Sometimes user wants force INLA dispatch despite refusal — typically done homework know silent-bias risk. override two-key armed: requires force = "inla", acknowledge_silent_bias_risk = TRUE, non-empty reason: override leaves audit trail $capabilities: literal reason string rule(s) bypassed. Downstream tooling (triangulate() print method, particular) flags forced-INLA fits override visible cross-engine comparison. override apply check 7. numerical failure inside INLA (mode finder converge, marginal likelihood finite, hyperparameter boundary) non-overridable. forced-INLA fit survives check 7 numerically OK; forced- INLA fit fails check 7 rejected even override active.
code: ovr <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(   fb_t_bad,   force                        = "inla",   acknowledge_silent_bias_risk = TRUE,   reason                       = "exploratory: comparing with greta-fit posterior" ) class(ovr)            # fb_terms (overrideed) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list" grep("lgm_force", ovr$capabilities, value = TRUE) #> [1] "lgm_force_overridden"                                             #> [2] "lgm_force_reason:exploratory: comparing with greta-fit posterior" #> [3] "lgm_force_bypassed:family_allowlist"

what: 6. Capability flags beyond the gate verdict
title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: fb_terms$capabilities character vector flows dispatch infrastructure structured trail evidence. Beyond gate-verdict flags (lgm_compatible, lgm_force_overridden, soft-warning markers, override audit), every LGM-compatible IR also carries gretaR_slot_dormant — informational capability flag advertising package provisioned slot gretaR backend slot yet activated. slot dormant gretaR package yet publicly available package’s audit process new backends yet wired registry. Activation land subsequent release conditions met. , gretaR_status() canonical discoverability surface users see flag fb_terms$capabilities print method fitted model. user-facing backend argument flexybayes() / fb() advertise "gretaR" slot dormant; selection set c("auto", "greta", "inla", "brms") keeps surface honest. activated, slot widen set include "gretaR", swap canonical-name mapper stub production mapper, replace dispatch-helper branch real emit step.
code: gs <- gretaR_status() gs$dormancy_reason #> [1] "slot_provisioned_not_activated" cat(gs$activation_procedure, sep = "\n") #> Install the gretaR package when it goes public on CRAN. #> Verify the local gretaR build against the audit checklist. #> Run options(flexyBayes.gretaR_activated = TRUE) in the session.

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: Mistaking prior σ\sigma prior latent field. Student-tt prior sigma (random-effect standard deviation) fine trigger refusal — sigma hyperparameter, part latent field. Student-tt prior random-effect coefficient vector (exposed DSL, constructed manually ) trigger refusal, correctly. Reading “lgm_compatible” “model correct”. flag means INLA’s structural assumptions violated; mean model fits data, priors appropriate, posterior close truth. priors regularisation cross-engine triangulation vignettes cover orthogonal concerns. Treating refusal bug. Refusals package prevents silent approximation. right response one : use greta backend (backend = "greta"), reduce model complexity (e.g., factor-analytic instead unstructured), — understand risk — invoke override.

title: Tutorial 11: LGM feasibility: what `lgm_gate()` accepts and refuses
text: Construct fb_terms object family = "tweedie" (real distribution, supported INLA) run lgm_gate(). pass? ? Construct fb_terms fa(env, 2):gen yan.winterwheat. pass budget check? hyperparameter count? Run forced-INLA fit refused model compare posterior greta-backend fit via triangulate(). override intended surface bias; visible ?

Approximate Bayesian inference latent Gaussian models using integrated nested Laplace approximations. Journal Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 71(2), 319–392. Simpson, D., Rue, H., Riebler, ., Martins, T. G., & Sørbye, S. H. (2017). Penalising model component complexity: principled, practical approach constructing priors. Statistical Science, 32(1), 1–28.

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: Every flexybayes(), fb_brms(), fb_greta() call produces fitted object plus, $extras slot, literal program package generated chosen backend (applicable). Reading program fastest way understand: random-effect parameterisation package chose; prior translates greta INLA; cross-engine equivalence lives; extend package new backend. vignette reference power users, contributors, anyone debugging unexpected backend choice refusal.

what: 2. The dispatch flow at a glance
title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: fb_terms class intermediate representation (IR) package. Everything downstream — lgm_gate, emit shims, posterior unification — operates fb_terms, user’s formulas. separation allows two ingest paths (asreml-format brms-format) share single dispatch infrastructure.
code: user call   → fb_from_brms()  or  fb_from_asreml()        (both produce a single internal class: fb_terms)   → lgm_gate(fb_terms)        │        ├ pass    → emit_greta()  or  emit_inla()        └ refuse  → lgm_refusal (printed to user; user re-routes)   → fitted-model object (class flexybayes or flexybayes_inla)

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: IR S3-classed list twelve fields; purpose debugging, four essential. print method gives one-page digest: four fields read often: $response — response variable name (string). $family — response family (string family object). $fixed_terms, $random_terms, $rcov_terms — lists term descriptors. descriptor names type (e.g., "factor", "continuous", "simple", "fa_gxe"), variables touches, term-specific parameters. $capabilities — character vector populated lgm_gate() dispatch. presence "lgm_compatible" means model passed six structural checks.
code: library(flexyBayes) data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4") fb_t <- flexyBayes:::fb_from_brms(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject),   data = sleepstudy ) class(fb_t) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list" names(fb_t) #>  [1] "response"       "family"         "link"           "intercept"      #>  [5] "fixed_terms"    "random_terms"   "rcov_terms"     "addition_terms" #>  [9] "priors"         "data_summary"   "capabilities"   "source"         #> [13] "greta_meta" fb_t #> <fb_terms> flexyBayes intermediate representation #>   source:    brms #>   response:  Reaction #>   family:    gaussian (identity link) #>   intercept: TRUE #>   terms: #>     fixed:    1 #>     random:   1 #>     rcov:     0 #>     addition: 0 #>   priors:    <user-supplied> #>   capabilities: <not yet evaluated by lgm_gate>

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: fb_from_asreml() walks fixed/random/rcov formulas asreml-format call: fb_from_brms() walks single brms-style formula via brms::brmsterms() brms loaded; otherwise parses minimum subset directly: $source field carries "asreml" "brms" surfaces triangulate() reports cross-engine comparisons disclose ingest path fit.
code: data(met_example, package = "flexyBayes") fb_a <- flexyBayes:::fb_from_asreml(   fixed = yield ~ env,   random = ~ fa(env, 2):geno,   rcov = NULL,   data = met_example$dat ) fb_a$random_terms[[1]]$type #> [1] "fa_gxe" fb_a$random_terms[[1]]$k         # number of factor-analytic loadings #> [1] 2 fb_b <- flexyBayes:::fb_from_brms(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject),   data = sleepstudy ) fb_b$source                       # "brms" #> [1] "brms" fb_b$random_terms[[1]]$type       # "simple" #> [1] "simple"

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: emit_greta() emit_inla() package’s two backend shims. consume fb_terms IR return fitted-model object. emit_greta() calls existing parse_formula → codegen → build_glm pipeline (legacy flexyBayes engine, retained verbatim compatibility). job unwrap IR back shape legacy code expects, run fit, wrap result. emit_inla() builds INLA formula f(group, model = "iid", hyper = ...) random effect, plumbs priors_to_inla() hyperparameter slot, calls INLA::inla(), runs check 7 — post-fit numerical- confirm gate. literal greta program available fit: output literal greta program: greta::normal(), greta::uniform(), model definition, greta::mcmc() call. Reading removes much magic. INLA, formula passed INLA::inla() fit_inla$inla$.args$formula:
code: fit <- fb_greta(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,   n_samples    = 200, warmup = 200, chains = 1,   verbose      = FALSE,   mcmc_verbose = FALSE ) cat(fit$extras$code, sep = "\n") #> # -- Fixed effects ------------------------------------------- #> mu_atg <- normal(0, 100) #> beta_Days <- normal(0, 100) #> # -- Random effects ------------------------------------------ #> sigma_Subject <- greta::uniform(0, 281.6438) #> Subject_raw <- normal(0, 1, dim = n_Subject) #> u_Subject <- Subject_raw * sigma_Subject #> # -- Residual ------------------------------------------------ #> sigma_e_atg <- greta::uniform(0, 281.6438) #> # -- Linear predictor ---------------------------------------- #> mu_i_atg <- mu_atg + beta_Days * as_data(Days) + u_Subject[Subject_id] #> # -- Likelihood ---------------------------------------------- #> y_atg_obs <- as_data(y_atg) #> distribution(y_atg_obs) <- normal(mu_i_atg, sigma_e_atg) #> # -- Model and MCMC ----------------------------------------- #> atg_model <- greta::model(mu_atg, beta_Days, sigma_Subject, sigma_e_atg) #> atg_draws <- greta::mcmc(atg_model, n_samples = 200, warmup = 200, chains = 1, verbose = FALSE) fit_inla <- fb_inla(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,   verbose = FALSE ) fit_inla$inla$.args$formula #> Reaction ~ 1 + Days + f(Subject, model = "iid", hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "pc.prec",  #>     param = c(281.643785626176, 0.01)))) #> NULL

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: lgm_gate() structural-first feasibility filter decides whether INLA can fit IR. LGM feasibility vignette catalogues check override path; section examines call package side. return value one three: IR "lgm_compatible" appended $capabilities — proceed emit_inla(); IR "lgm_force_overridden" appended — proceed emit_inla() despite refusal, user authorisation; structured lgm_refusal object — print stop. Inside flexybayes(..., backend = "auto") (equivalently fb(...), whose default backend "auto"), dispatch logic literally: refusal branch, dispatch falls back greta emits one-time silenceable note carrying primary failure rule (options(flexyBayes.silence_auto_fallback_note = TRUE)). INLA installed, auto dispatch routes greta separate silenceable note (options(flexyBayes.silence_auto_inla_missing_note = TRUE)). full trace available every fit [backend_decision()]: returned list reports backend (backend ran), path ("direct", "auto_inla_accepted", "auto_inla_refused", etc.), gate_checks (list structural checks ran), reason (free text).
code: res <- flexyBayes:::lgm_gate(fb_b) class(res) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list" res$capabilities #> [1] "lgm_compatible"      "gretaR_slot_dormant" if (inherits(res, "fb_terms"))   return(emit_inla(res, data = data, ...))      # gate accepted if (inherits(res, "lgm_refusal"))   return(emit_greta(res$fb, data = data, ...))  # gate refused fit_auto <- flexybayes(   Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,   backend      = "auto",   n_samples    = 200, warmup = 200, chains = 1,   verbose      = FALSE,   mcmc_verbose = FALSE ) backend_decision(fit_auto) #> $backend #> [1] "inla" #>  #> $path #> [1] "auto_accept" #>  #> $gate_checks #> [1] "lgm_compatible"      "gretaR_slot_dormant" #>  #> $reason #> [1] "lgm_gate() accepted; INLA dispatch." #>  #> $preflight_summary #> NULL #>  #> $representation_plan #> NULL #>  #> $rejected_routes #> $rejected_routes[[1]] #> $rejected_routes[[1]]$backend #> [1] "greta" #>  #> $rejected_routes[[1]]$reason #> [1] "not_chosen_by_policy" #>  #>  #> $rejected_routes[[2]] #> $rejected_routes[[2]]$backend #> [1] "gretaR" #>  #> $rejected_routes[[2]]$reason #> [1] "backend_not_activated" #>  #>  #>  #> $routing_policy_version #> [1] "stage5a_v1"

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: fb_terms$capabilities character vector flows dispatch infrastructure structured trail evidence. carries: "lgm_compatible" (passed six structural checks); soft warnings ("lgm_warning:hyperparam_budget:soft"); override audit ("lgm_force_bypassed:family_allowlist", "lgm_force_reason:..."); ingest provenance ($source field, separate always read capabilities). Every triangulate() print scans markers surfaces prominently cross-engine comparisons disclose dispatch context.

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: fb_greta() expert / power-user entry. Use model already expressed native greta primitives (as_data(), normal(), uniform(), distribution()<-, model()) want flexyBayes post-fit surface — summary(), coef(), vcov(), prior_summary(), triangulate() — without rewriting model asreml brms-style formula grammar. canonical_names argument fb_from_greta() maps greta target-array name canonical brms-style name flexyBayes uses elsewhere ((Intercept), Days, sigma, sd_<group>, …). mapping unlocks triangulate() flexyBayes fit model without user-supplied name_map. canonical_names omitted package falls back verbatim greta names one-time structured note. fb_greta() deliberately never re-priors user’s model: greta program source truth. Pass fb_prior() object declaration (downstream prior_summary() / triangulate() reporting); semantic mismatch declared prior model graph raises structural refusal. Two flags deferred fb_greta() path: return_code = TRUE (user’s model already greta-side, flexyBayes-generated code string return) review_code = TRUE (model-graph pretty-printer deferred-execution token queued subsequent release). formula-entry verbs (flexybayes() / fb_brms()) continue support flags.
code: library(greta) data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4")  y      <- as_data(sleepstudy$Reaction) x_days <- as_data(sleepstudy$Days)  b0    <- normal(0, 100) b1    <- normal(0, 100) sigma <- uniform(0, 5 * sd(sleepstudy$Reaction))  mu <- b0 + b1 * x_days distribution(y) <- normal(mu, sigma)  m <- model(b0, b1, sigma)  fit_dg <- fb_greta(   fb_from_greta(     m,     canonical_names = c(b0 = "(Intercept)", b1 = "Days",                         sigma = "sigma")   ),   n_samples       = 200,   warmup          = 200,   chains          = 1,   verbose         = FALSE,   mcmc_verbose    = FALSE ) coef(fit_dg) #> (Intercept)        Days       sigma  #>   252.12209    10.37889    48.11216

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: package carries slot gretaR backend alongside greta INLA. slot registered canonical-name registry, lgm_gate(), shared dispatch helper, advertised user-facing backend argument activated. Activation lands subsequent release gretaR package publicly available cleared package’s audit gate. activation, every LGM-compatible IR carries informational capability flag gretaR_slot_dormant, code path dispatched gretaR (reachable internal callers tests today) raises structured gretaR_dormant_refusal error naming dormancy reason activation procedure. Activation four-touch-point flip: widen match.arg sets user-facing entries, swap canonical-name mapper stub production mapper, replace dispatch-helper branch body real emit step, update lgm_gate capability flag value.
code: gretaR_status() #> $activated #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $gretaR_installed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $audit_clean #> [1] NA #>  #> $dormancy_reason #> [1] "slot_provisioned_not_activated" #>  #> $activation_procedure #> [1] "Install the gretaR package when it goes public on CRAN."         #> [2] "Verify the local gretaR build against the audit checklist."      #> [3] "Run options(flexyBayes.gretaR_activated = TRUE) in the session."

title: Tutorial 12: Backend internals: greta, INLA, and the intermediate representation
text: package architecture wrap-don’t-rewrite — legacy greta pipeline preserved verbatim, new backends sit alongside . Adding third backend (Stan via brms passthrough; PyMC via reticulate; …) follows five-step recipe: Implement emit_<backend>() function takes fb_terms IR plus user-facing arguments returns fitted-model object. Register new backend match.arg(backend, …) set universal entries flexybayes() / fb() (engine pins fb_greta() / fb_inla() / fb_brms() fix single backend take backend argument). Decide gating policy — new backend require lgm_compatible? Add check entry emit_<backend>. Implement fb_as_draws_simple method fitted-model class, returning named list draw vectors. unlocks triangulate() immediately. Register canonical-name mapper via internal register_canonical_mapper() helper triangulate() can reconcile parameter names existing greta INLA mappers without user-supplied name_map. Write tests + vignette demonstrating new path. cross-engine triangulation vignette pattern template. third emit backend already live: Stan passthrough via brms (fb_brms(), flexybayes(..., backend = "brms")), whose canonical-name mapper identity brms. Two provisioned planned: gretaR (slot provisioned dormant current release; see §9 ), NIMBLE (hidden Markov, multistate, partial-LGM models INLA refuses benefit NIMBLE’s specialised samplers).

title: Tutorial 13: Per-term INLA memory feasibility
text: lgm_gate() rule 11 (memory_feasibility_inla) originally used single multiplier preflight’s indexed estimate. multiplier useful safety net coarse: model whose memory profile dominated one structured-covariance term refuse wrong reason, diagnostic message named neither binding term representation class driving projection. gate now uses per-term decomposition instead. random-effect term contributes bytes according representation class: fixed-effect contribution design matrix’s N x p * 8 bytes. grand total multiplied overhead factor (default 2.0; configurable via flexyBayes.preflight_inla_overhead_factor option) absorbs INLA’s internal stage-2 allocations.

title: Tutorial 13: Per-term INLA memory feasibility
text: fb_plan() flexybayes(plan = TRUE) surface breakdown headline memory line plan’s print form. underlying data sits plan$preflight$memory_estimate: shape stable: field names follow closed vocabulary. .numeric(plan$preflight$memory_estimate) returns total bytes, existing scalar-numeric consumers continue work unchanged.
code: List of 4  $ total          : num <total bytes after overhead>  $ breakdown      :'data.frame': <K + 1> obs. of  4 variables:   ..$ term_label    : chr  <one row per random term + "(fixed effects)">   ..$ representation: chr  <representation class>   ..$ bytes         : num  <bytes>   ..$ share         : num  <share of raw total>  $ overhead_factor: num 2  $ raw_total      : num <bytes before overhead>  - attr(*, "class") = c("fb_memory_estimate", "list")

what: 3. How to negotiate a memory refusal
title: Tutorial 13: Per-term INLA memory feasibility
text: per-term estimator’s total exceeds active ceiling, lgm_gate() refuses memory_feasibility_inla_per_term names largest contributor condition’s diagnostic line. Four common patterns resolve refusal: Route via greta. backend = "greta" keeps indexed representation throughout MCMC; dense materialisation INLA’s stage-2 needs bypassed entirely. Reduce structured-cov cardinality. Aggregate levels grouping factor science permits (e.g., site x season instead site x date). Switch dense sparse precision. structured covariance known sparse precision, pass precision = solve(V) instead V term routes INLA’s sparse generic0 path. Raise ceiling. Pass memory_ceiling_gb (set flexyBayes.preflight_ram_fraction) machine genuinely headroom per-term breakdown reads honestly.

what: 4. What the per-term estimator does not capture
title: Tutorial 13: Per-term INLA memory feasibility
text: per-term estimator covers four exact representations flexyBayes supports today (dense, chol, precision/pedigree-sparse, blocks) plus indexed random-effect dense-basis fixed-effect shapes. Two surfaces explicitly scope: SPDE meshes. INLA’s model = "spde2" mesh allocations workflow-specific modelled ; future release ships tensor-smooth estimator covers relevant shapes. Approximate schemes (low-rank covariance carriers, variational stage-2). refuse routing layer validated validate_approximation().

title: Choosing an engine: the universal entry and the engine pins
text: fb() (full-name twin flexybayes()) universal entry. detects input grammar call shape, builds backend-agnostic intermediate representation, dispatches backend. -backend call uses backend = "auto", routes INLA model latent-Gaussian feasible greta otherwise, single call reaches whichever engine model supports. grammar read call, never guessed intent. bar-grouped formula Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject) read brms grammar; bar-free fixed formula paired random / rcov ASReml grammar. lone ambiguous case – bare y ~ x grouping, means model either way – defaults ASReml. Force reading syntax:
code: data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4")  # brms / lme4-style grammar (a bar-grouped formula): detected automatically. fit <- fb(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy)  # ASReml grammar: fixed / random / rcov. fit_asreml <- fb(   fixed  = Reaction ~ Days,   random = ~ Subject,   data   = sleepstudy ) # Treat a bar-free formula as brms grammar explicitly. fit_brms_grammar <- fb(Reaction ~ Days, data = sleepstudy, syntax = "brms")

what: Letting auto choose, and reading its decision
title: Choosing an engine: the universal entry and the engine pins
text: backend = "auto" call consults latent-Gaussian gate. model feasible INLA installed, fits via INLA (fast, deterministic Laplace approximation). Otherwise falls back greta (full Hamiltonian Monte Carlo), one-time note. recorded decision – including engines considered chosen – available fit: preview routing without fitting, pass plan = TRUE, returns planning object describing chosen backend, representation plan, prediction plan.
code: fit <- fb(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,           backend = "auto") backend_decision(fit) fb(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy, plan = TRUE)

title: Choosing an engine: the universal entry and the engine pins
text: want specific engine, pins make intent explicit. exactly fb() backend fixed, accepts grammars arguments universal entry: pin fits via one engine , conflicting backend argument clear error rather silent override: every engine can represent every model. brms / Stan engine, example, represent ASReml structured-covariance term (vm, ped, fa, us, ar1) low_rank smooth approximation. request raises structured refusal names offending construct points engine can fit – package never silently fits different model one asked .
code: fit_g <- fb_greta(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy) fit_i <- fb_inla(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject),  data = sleepstudy) fit_s <- fb_brms(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject),  data = sleepstudy) # Refused: fb_inla() pins INLA, so it cannot also take backend = "greta". fb_inla(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,         backend = "greta")

title: Choosing an engine: the universal entry and the engine pins
text: already built model native greta primitives, pass greta::model() object straight fb(). fit directly greta::mcmc() returned standard post-fit surface (summary(), coef(), vcov(), triangulate()). align native fit INLA Stan fit triangulate(), parameters need canonical names. Attach building intermediate representation fb_from_greta(), pass result fb(): universal entry gains extra argument : canonical map travels intermediate representation, fb_from_*() adapters place prepare specification fitting.
code: library(greta) y     <- as_data(sleepstudy$Reaction) x     <- as_data(sleepstudy$Days) b0    <- normal(0, 100) b1    <- normal(0, 100) sigma <- uniform(0, 5 * sd(sleepstudy$Reaction)) distribution(y) <- normal(b0 + b1 * x, sigma) m <- model(b0, b1, sigma)  fit_native <- fb(m) ir <- fb_from_greta(   m,   canonical_names = c(b0 = "(Intercept)", b1 = "Days", sigma = "sigma") ) fit_native <- fb(ir)

title: Big data: streaming exact aggregation to a billion rows
text: realistic delivery format columnar file disk. flexybayes_stream() reads row-range chunks, accumulates sufficient statistics, fits KK-cell model INLA (greta). Peak memory one chunk plus small cell accumulator, never full table. dataset shared several partitioned shards – usual shape genuinely large data – pass vector shard paths streamed turn bounded footprint. Binomial poisson models fit way. 0/1 response treated Bernoulli (one trial per row). poisson, name exposure column exposure = data carries one. inspect compression design achieve committing fit, pass fit = FALSE function returns aggregation summary.
code: library(fst)  # A dataset already on disk (here written for illustration). path <- "trial.fst"  fit <- flexybayes_stream(   yield ~ environment,         # fixed factor(s)   random = ~ genotype,         # random-intercept grouping factor   source = path,               # an .fst path on disk   family = "gaussian",   backend = "inla",   chunk_rows = 5e6             # rows read per chunk )  summary(fit) shards <- sort(list.files("trial_parts", pattern = "\\.fst$",                           full.names = TRUE)) fit <- flexybayes_stream(   yield ~ environment, random = ~ genotype,   source = shards, family = "gaussian", backend = "inla" ) fit_bin <- flexybayes_stream(   infected ~ region, random = ~ paddock,   source = "survey.fst", family = "binomial", backend = "inla" )  fit_pois <- flexybayes_stream(   count ~ treatment, random = ~ site,   source = "counts.fst", family = "poisson", backend = "inla" ) agg <- flexybayes_stream(   yield ~ environment, random = ~ genotype,   source = path, fit = FALSE ) agg          # prints N, K, and the compression ratio

ASReml lme4 fit ; flexyBayes yet. package’s central open problem. See inst/KNOWN_ISSUES.md (system.file("KNOWN_ISSUES.md", package = "flexyBayes")) full status, per-backend reasons, intended route fix. , flexyBayes honest multi-backend orchestration triangulation tool model classes – yet stand-alone Bayesian replacement full ASReml-style MET analysis. Breeder MET summaries. fb_met_summary() (overall performance, stability, GxE BLUPs, factor loadings, environment genetic correlations) requires greta factor-analytic (fa(env, k):gen) fit — computed identified realised effects, fit greta. INLA brms fit refuses pointer right path; scalable INLA MET route gives variance components via summary() / fb_structured_cov().

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: flexyBayes degrades gracefully optional engine missing: backend detected run time, model sent unavailable engine refused clear message naming install rather failing obscurely.
code: # greta (R-native MCMC backend) — archived from CRAN, served via the # greta-dev R-universe install.packages("greta",   repos = c("https://greta-dev.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos"))) greta::install_greta_deps()  # INLA (approximate-inference backend) — not on CRAN install.packages("INLA",   repos = c(getOption("repos"),             INLA = "https://inla.r-inla-download.org/R/stable"))  # brms (Stan passthrough) — on CRAN install.packages("brms")  # flexyBayes itself (not yet on CRAN) -- install from the repository: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("AAGI-AUS/flexyBayes")

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: planner needs inference backend fastest way see flexyBayes model: builds intermediate representation, chooses backend, reports plan without fitting. fit, install least one backend (see Backend support ). following uses production sampling settings; getting started vignette walks fit convergence diagnostics.
code: library(flexyBayes) data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4")  # Inspect the routing decision and representation plan -- no backend needed plan <- flexybayes(   fixed  = Reaction ~ Days,   random = ~ Subject,   data   = sleepstudy,   plan   = TRUE ) plan fit <- flexybayes(   fixed  = Reaction ~ Days,   random = ~ Subject,   data   = sleepstudy,   n_samples = 2000, warmup = 5000, chains = 4 )  # Standard R output summary(fit) coef(fit) confint(fit)  # emmeans + marginaleffects emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ Days, at = list(Days = c(0, 5))) marginaleffects::avg_slopes(fit)

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: Fit model two backends compare: Add third engine via brms / Stan: canonical_names() work aligning backend-native parameter names (greta’s sigma_g, INLA’s Precision g precision scale, brms’s sd_g__Intercept) single canonical name correct scale transform — name_map argument needed standard cases.
code: fit_g <- flexybayes(Reaction ~ Days, random = ~ Subject,                     data = sleepstudy, backend = "greta") fit_i <- flexybayes(Reaction ~ Days, random = ~ Subject,                     data = sleepstudy, backend = "inla")  triangulate(fit_g, fit_i) #> Common parameters: 3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   parameter   mean_diff sd_ratio tail_drift wasserstein_1 #>   <chr>           <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)    -0.04     0.997     0.012          0.04 #> 2 Days            0.001    1.001     0.003          0.001 #> 3 sigma           0.12     1.004     0.020          0.12 fit_s <- fb_brms(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy)  triangulate(fit_g, fit_s) triangulate(fit_i, fit_s)

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: Every fit carries three (four) top-level slots:
code: fit$glm         # GLM-compatible shim — works with summary(), emmeans,                 # marginaleffects, effectsize, broom  fit$greta       # native greta output (when backend = "greta") fit$inla        # native INLA output (when backend = "inla") fit$brms        # live brmsfit (when backend = "brms")  fit$extras      # BLUPs, variance components, convergence diagnostics,                 # generated code, parsed IR, run time, captured call

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
code: # Fixed effects yield ~ env                  # fixed factor yield ~ env + x_cov          # factor + covariate yield ~ 0 + env              # means model (no intercept) yield ~ env + I(x^2)         # expression terms  # Random effects random = ~ geno                       # simple iid random = ~ block:rep                  # nested random = ~ vm(geno, Gmat)             # GBLUP (dense V; both backends) random = ~ vm(geno, chol = L)         # user-supplied Cholesky (greta only; v0.3.7+) random = ~ vm(geno, precision = Q)    # user-supplied sparse precision (INLA preferred; v0.3.7+) random = ~ ped(animal, Amat)          # pedigree (animal model) random = ~ ped(animal, A_inv,                use_sparse_precision = TRUE)  # sparse pedigree precision (v0.3.7+) random = ~ at(env):geno               # diagonal GxE random = ~ us(env):id(geno)           # unstructured GxE random = ~ fa(env, 2):id(geno)        # factor-analytic GxE random = ~ ar1(row):id(col)           # spatial AR1 random = ~ spl(x_cov)                 # P-spline  # Residual rcov = ~ units                # iid residuals (default) rcov = ~ at(env):units        # heterogeneous by environment  # Families family = "gaussian" | "binomial" | "poisson" | "negative_binomial" |          "gamma" | "beta"

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: Sixteen numbered vignettes ship package: Getting started ASReml-shaped formulas Foundational regression Hierarchical models Structured covariance Priors regularisation METs genomic selection Downstream analysis Spatio-temporal models Cross-engine triangulation LGM feasibility filter Backend internals LGM feasibility memory Choosing engine: universal entry engine pins Architecture: backend registry new engine joins Big-data streaming Heavy MCMC vignettes use .Rmd.orig precompile pattern; .Rmd ships package tarball pre-evaluated static output. Browse browseVignettes("flexyBayes") full install — R CMD build R CMD INSTALL tarball, devtools::install(build_vignettes = TRUE). plain install_github() source-directory install build vignettes inst/doc. Several reference vignettes (02, 04, 05, 07) fit small sampling budgets print diagnostics meet convergence thresholds; opens “illustration output shape, inference” disclaimer. convergence-clean workflow see getting started cross-engine triangulation vignettes.

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: Indexing, model matrices: parameters sized number levels (p), observations (N), giving O(p) memory random effects. Non-centred parameterisation: random effects use NCP efficient MCMC sampling. Cholesky decomposition: known covariance matrices (G, ) decomposed . Triangulation first-class output: package built around claim evidence cross-engine agreement signature flexyBayes deliverable, “one Bayesian mixed- model frontend”.

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: flexyBayes ships extensive testthat suite (devtools::test()) covering paths likely hide errors: fixed-effect factor models lm() / lme4::lmer() references, random intercepts asreml-route random slopes, structured-covariance terms, streaming-aggregation equivalence per-row fit, weights, offsets, missing-response handling, backend routing structured refusal taxonomy, prior translation, cross-engine triangulate() agreement. cross-engine triangulation per-family vignettes show clean reproducible checks; structured-covariance reference vignettes explicitly marked non-inferential model mix vignette-scale budgets.

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: Continuous integration validates INLA, brms, engine-independent surface (ASReml / brms parsers, intermediate representation, lgm_gate(), dispatch policy table, refusal registry, prior DSL, triangulate() metrics). greta integration path (Python + TensorFlow) installed standard CI matrix, green CI run include live greta fits; greta-dependent tests gate via skip_if_no_greta() run locally / via manual greta-integration check. Run full suite locally devtools::test() greta::install_greta_deps().

title: flexyBayes — Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
text: flexyBayes refuses yet fit rather fitting silently. current release cover following; roadmap deferral, request needs one met structured refusal naming gap, quiet wrong answer. Full multi-environment-trial (MET) models: genotype--environment interaction random effects (gen:loc, gen:loc:yearf) heteroscedastic per-environment residuals (dsum(~ units | env)) fittable trustworthy backend release – INLA brms refuse , greta expresses converge . package’s central open problem; see inst/KNOWN_ISSUES.md full status intended route fix. Use ASReml lme4 full MET model today. Spatial structure: separable AR1 (ar1(row):id(col)) supported. Intrinsic CAR BYM2 areal models implemented. can express custom spatial precision passing matrix vm(g, precision = Q), BYM2 helper. Smooth terms: univariate penalised splines (s(x), spl(x)) supported. Multivariate tensor-product smooths (te(), ti(), t2()) refused deferred later release. Hidden-Markov, multi-state, survival models: supported. Survival / time--event families refused family gate. NIMBLE backend covering roadmap fixed release target. Missing data: flexyBayes impute. Greta brms (Stan) passthrough require complete cases model variables; INLA treats NA response prediction target. Resolve missingness (drop impute) fitting, use prediction path deliberately.

title: Backend dispatch trace for a flexyBayes fit — backend_decision
text: list following components. first four present every fit; four routing-trace fields present v0.3.6+ fits NULL earlier fits backward compatibility. backend Character; one "greta", "inla", "brms", "gretaR". path Character; dispatch path token. gate_checks List NULL; lgm_gate() check trail (failures refusal; capabilities accept). reason Character; dispatch-decision rationale. preflight_summary <fb_preflight> object NULL. Populated .fb_preflight() ran (>1e5-row path); NULL small-data fast path. representation_plan Named list slim per-term entries (term_id, representation_class, justification) derived preflight_summary; NULL preflight. rejected_routes List (backend, reason) pairs backends considered chosen. Empty explicit user requests (routing policy bypassed backend named directly). routing_policy_version Character; e.g. "stage5a_v1". audit-anchor reproducibility – policy change bumps string.

text: Engine pin: fits model Stan brms . sugar flexybayes()(..., backend = "brms") accepts arguments grammars — ASReml fixed / random / rcov specification brms-style bar-grouped formula (see flexybayes() full argument list). flexyBayes builds intermediate representation, translates prior, calls brms::brm(), wraps result; live brmsfit available $brms slot brms's posterior tooling (loo(), posterior_predict(), bayes_factor()).

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the brms (Stan) engine — fb_brms
code: fb_brms(...)

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the brms (Stan) engine — fb_brms
text: ... Arguments passed flexybayes() (e.g. formula / fixed, random, rcov, data, family, prior, syntax). backend argument pinned "brms"; conflicting backend value raises structured refusal (redundant backend = "brms" accepted). pre-v0.5.0 formula =  argument remapped universal entry's model-spec slot call-compatibility.

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the brms (Stan) engine — fb_brms
text: object class "flexybayes_brms" (subclass "flexybayes") carrying live brmsfit $brms; see flexybayes() shared structure.

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the brms (Stan) engine — fb_brms
text: brms / Stan engine represent ASReml structured-covariance term (vm, ped, fa, us, ar1) low_rank smooth approximation; model raises structured refusal naming offending construct. Re-fit fb_greta() (full MCMC) , model latent-Gaussian feasible, fb_inla().

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the brms (Stan) engine — fb_brms
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("brms", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4")   fit <- fb_brms(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,                  chains = 1)   coef(fit) } # }

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
text: Wraps known covariance, precision, Cholesky factor, block-diagonal list, low-rank factor classed carrier object structured random-effect terms consume, directly vm(geno, cov = fb_cov(G, type = "chol")). v0.4.0 constructor-noun replacement legacy vm() keyword forms (chol = , precision = , blocks = , low_rank_factor = ), deprecate release removed v0.5.0.

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
code: fb_cov(M, type = "dense", levels = NULL, scheme = NULL, ...)

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
text: M covariance carrier. type = "blocks" base-R list K square covariance matrices; otherwise square numeric matrix (base-R Matrix). Inside formula argument names matrix resolved known_matrices. type Character(1): carrier type, one "dense", "chol", "precision", "blocks", "low_rank". Defaults "dense". levels Optional character vector grouping-factor level labels carrier's rows / columns align . Carried metadata; fit-time validator checks alignment fitted factor. scheme Character(1), required type = "low_rank": name registered approximation scheme (see validate_approximation()). Ignored exact types. ... Reserved forward carrier-type options; currently carried verbatim object.

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
text: fb_cov object: classed list matrix M, type, optional levels, (low-rank) scheme elements, plus representation_class validation_summary attributes.

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
text: carrier's type selects downstream emit path derives covariance square root. "dense" takes lower Cholesky supplied covariance; "chol" uses supplied factor directly; "precision" inverts via Cholesky precision matrix; "blocks" assembles block-diagonal covariance list per-block matrices; "low_rank" pairs rank-K factor registered approximation scheme (reserved v0.4.0 – carrier vocabulary active validated, approximate-covariance fit route activates subsequent release). Inside model formula carrier written inline – matrix argument names matrix passed known_matrices, exactly legacy keyword forms . construction-time check light structural probe (shape, lower-triangular pattern, symmetry, block count); full level-aware validation runs fit time grouping factor.

title: Construct a structured-covariance carrier — fb_cov
code: G <- crossprod(matrix(rnorm(9L), 3L, 3L)) fb_cov(G, type = "dense") #> <fb_cov> type = "dense" #>   representation: dense_cov #>   carrier: dense: 3x3 L <- t(chol(G)) fb_cov(L, type = "chol") #> <fb_cov> type = "chol" #>   representation: chol_cov #>   carrier: chol: 3x3 lower-triangular

what: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
text: Estimates concentration vector \(\alpha\) Dirichlet distribution matrix compositional rows (row composition simplex). default method maximum likelihood via base optim() (dependency-free, deterministic); Bayesian alternative uses greta's native dirichlet distribution. routes return fb_dirichlet_fit shape, tidy() summary identical across methods.

title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
code: fb_dirichlet(   x,   method = c("ml", "greta"),   conf_level = 0.95,   eps = 1e-06,   n_samples = 2000L,   warmup = 1000L,   chains = 4L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
text: x numeric matrix data.frame compositional rows: least two columns, entries non-negative, least four rows. method Character. "ml" maximum likelihood (default) "greta" Bayesian route. conf_level Numeric (0, 1). interval level parameter summary. Defaults 0.95. eps Numeric. boundary nudge applied exact zeros / ones. Defaults 1e-06. n_samples, warmup, chains Integer MCMC controls "greta" method (ignored "ml"). Default 2000, 1000, 4. verbose Logical. Whether greta route prints sampler progress. Defaults FALSE.

title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
text: object class c("fb_dirichlet_fit", "fb_family_fit"): list estimates (data.frame term / estimate / std.error / conf.low / conf.high, one row per component), mean_composition (numeric, summing one), method, n_obs, n_components, logLik (ML ), draws (greta ).

title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
text: Rows renormalised sum one, exact zeros ones nudged inside open simplex small eps (Dirichlet density -Inf simplex boundary). maximum-likelihood route optimises log concentrations keep positive recovers standard errors Hessian delta method. Bayesian route places independent half-normal priors concentrations reports posterior means quantile-based credible intervals. fitted mean composition (alpha / sum(alpha)) reported fit$mean_composition.

title: Fit a Dirichlet distribution to compositional data — fb_dirichlet
Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
text: Names concrete inference engine tuning options. result passed backend argument fitting verbs: flexybayes(..., backend = fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L)). bare string form (backend = "greta") remains valid equivalent default fb_engine() engine.

title: Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
code: fb_engine(name, opts = list(), ...)

title: Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
text: name Character(1): engine, one "greta", "inla", "brms". opts Named list tuning options. Recognised names n_samples, warmup, chains; unrecognised name error. ... Tuning options given individually, merged opts (e.g. fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L)).

title: Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
text: fb_engine object: classed list elements name, paradigm (one mcmc, laplace, vb, map), toolchain_status (one ready, requires_install, unavailable), opts.

title: Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
text: name derived paradigm closed vocabularies. "auto" routing directive, engine; pass backend = "auto" directly automatic routing.

title: Construct an inference-engine specification — fb_engine
code: e <- fb_engine("greta", chains = 4L) e$paradigm #> [1] "mcmc" e$toolchain_status #> [1] "requires_install"

title: Dirichlet family object — fb_family_dirichlet
text: Constructs family descriptor Dirichlet distribution, natural model compositional (simplex) data. object mirrors shape flexyBayes family descriptors – named list carrying family name, parameter description, support – signalling Dirichlet fitted dedicated fb_dirichlet() entry point rather GLM-link emit path.

title: Dirichlet family object — fb_family_dirichlet
code: fb_family_dirichlet()

title: Dirichlet family object — fb_family_dirichlet
text: object class c("fb_family_dirichlet", "fb_family"): list family (canonical string "dirichlet"), parameters ("concentration (alpha), one per simplex component"), support ("K-simplex"), fitter ("fb_dirichlet").

title: Dirichlet family object — fb_family_dirichlet
text: Dirichlet \(K\)-simplex parameterised vector \(K\) positive concentration parameters \(\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_K\). sum controls concentration (large sum gives compositions tightly clustered around mean), normalised vector \(\alpha / \sum_k \alpha_k\) mean composition.

title: Dirichlet family object — fb_family_dirichlet
code: fam <- fb_family_dirichlet() fam$family #> [1] "dirichlet"

what: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
title: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
text: Constructs family descriptor generalised extreme value distribution, limiting law block maxima. object mirrors shape base stats::family() descriptor – named list carrying family name, parameter names, natural support – sits alongside flexyBayes families, signalling GEV fitted dedicated fb_gev() entry point rather GLM-link emit path.

title: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
code: fb_family_gev()

title: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
text: object class c("fb_family_gev", "fb_family"): list family (canonical string "gen_extreme_value"), parameters (character vector c("location", "scale", "shape")), n_par (integer 3), link ("identity" location), fitter ("fb_gev").

title: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
text: GEV parameterised location \(\mu\), positive scale \(\sigma\), shape \(\xi\) (extreme value index). shape governs tail: \(\xi > 0\) gives heavy-tailed Frechet type, \(\xi < 0\) bounded Weibull type, \(\xi \0\) Gumbel limit.

title: Generalised extreme value (GEV) family object — fb_family_gev
code: fam <- fb_family_gev() fam$parameters #> [1] "location" "scale"    "shape"

what: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
title: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
text: Parses ASReml-style fixed / random / rcov specification fb_terms object – flexyBayes's backend-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) model. IR every engine emits , building explicitly lets power user inspect parsed model, cache , hand fitting verb. users never call directly: flexybayes() fb() build IR internally arguments. Argument names defaults mirror flexybayes() one--one, drop-function's parsing step.

title: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
code: fb_from_asreml(   fixed,   random = NULL,   rcov = NULL,   data,   family = "gaussian",   link = NULL,   weights = NULL,   known_matrices = list(),   prior = NULL,   prior_fixed_sd = 100,   prior_vc_sd = 1 )

title: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
text: fixed Two-sided formula: response ~ fixed_effects. random One-sided formula ~ random_terms (ASReml syntax), NULL. rcov One-sided formula ~ residual_structure, NULL. NULL defaults iid residuals (list(list(type = "units"))), matching flexybayes(). data data.frame containing every variable referenced. family Character family name (gaussian, binomial, binary, poisson, negative_binomial, negbinom, gamma, beta). link Character link override, NULL family default. weights Optional numeric vector length nrow(data). non-NULL, mapped single addition term type = "weights". known_matrices Named list known matrices (e.g. list(Gmat = G)); names recorded data_summary$known_matrices. prior fb_prior() object, NULL. NULL falls back scalar priors prior_fixed_sd / prior_vc_sd. prior_fixed_sd Numeric SD fixed-effect normal priors prior NULL. prior_vc_sd Numeric hyperparameter variance-component priors prior NULL.

title: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
text: fb_terms object source = "asreml".

title: Ingest an ASReml-format model specification into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_asreml
code: df <- data.frame(   yield = rnorm(20),   geno  = factor(rep(letters[1:4], 5)),   env   = factor(rep(c("a", "b"), 10)) ) ir <- fb_from_asreml(yield ~ env, random = ~ geno, data = df) class(ir) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list"

what: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
text: Parses brms / lme4-style two-sided formula (response ~ fixed + (1 | g)) fb_terms object – flexyBayes's backend-agnostic intermediate representation (IR). IR every engine emits , building explicitly lets power user inspect parsed model, cache , hand fitting verb. users never call directly: fb() flexybayes() detect brms-style formula argument build IR internally.

title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
code: fb_from_brms(   formula,   data,   family = "gaussian",   link = NULL,   prior = NULL,   weights = NULL,   prior_fixed_sd = 100,   prior_vc_sd = 1,   carry_n_rows = NULL,   ... )

title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
text: formula base-R formula (recommended) brmsformula object. brmsformula support requires brms installed. data data.frame containing every referenced variable. May NULL advanced metadata-path (see carry_n_rows). family Character family name base-R family() object. link Character link override, NULL family default. prior fb_prior() object, NULL. weights Optional numeric weights vector length nrow(data), mapped single addition term type = "weights". prior_fixed_sd Numeric SD fixed-effect normal priors prior NULL. prior_vc_sd Numeric hyperparameter variance-component priors prior NULL. carry_n_rows Advanced: positive integer enabling metadata-IR path (data = NULL). formula's variables realised one-row placeholder row count recorded carry_n_rows, stress-testing preflight layer without materialising full data. Leave NULL ordinary use. ... Reserved future brms-ingest options (specials, autocor handling); currently unused.

title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
text: fb_terms object source = "brms".

title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
text: Linear fixed effects, random intercepts ((1 | g), (1 || g)) uncorrelated random intercept+slope ((x || g)) supported; correlated random slopes ((x | g)), smoothers, Gaussian processes autocorrelation terms refuse ingest structured message.

title: Ingest a brms-format formula into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_brms
code: df <- data.frame(   y = rnorm(30),   x = rnorm(30),   g = factor(rep(letters[1:5], 6)) ) ir <- fb_from_brms(y ~ x + (1 | g), data = df) class(ir) #> [1] "fb_terms" "list"

what: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
title: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
text: Wraps greta_model (built greta::model(...)) fb_terms object – flexyBayes's backend-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) – natively-specified greta graph can flow downstream machinery (summaries, triangulate(), canonical-name mapping) formula-ingested model. Unlike formula adapters post-hoc wrapper around already-built graph, resulting IR greta-construction: carries fixed / random / rcov term lists, populated greta_meta slot.

title: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
code: fb_from_greta(   model,   data = NULL,   prior = NULL,   canonical_names = NULL,   known_matrices = list() )

title: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
text: model greta_model returned greta::model(...). data Optional data.frame used build model, recorded IR downstream methods. required fitting – greta already captured data TensorFlow graph. prior Optional fb_prior() object. supplied, every name must resolve target greta_array; semantic agreement graph's encoded priors caller's responsibility. canonical_names Optional named character vector mapping greta-side parameter names canonical names. NULL falls back verbatim greta names (one-time silenceable note, since triangulate() non-greta backend needs canonical names). known_matrices Named list, mirroring ASReml entry; recorded data_summary$known_matrices.

title: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
text: fb_terms object source = "greta", intercept = NA, empty fixed / random / rcov term lists, populated greta_meta slot (carrying model graph, target arrays, canonical-name map). Pass returned IR straight fb() fit graph via greta keeping canonical-name map triangulate(): fb(fb_from_greta(model, canonical_names = ...)).

title: Ingest a user-built greta model into the flexyBayes IR — fb_from_greta
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(greta) mu    <- normal(0, 10) sigma <- normal(0, 5, truncation = c(0, Inf)) y     <- as_data(rnorm(20)) distribution(y) <- normal(mu, sigma) m  <- model(mu, sigma) ir <- fb_from_greta(m, canonical_names = c(mu = "(Intercept)")) fit <- fb(ir)   # fit the native graph via greta, keeping the map } # }

title: Genomic-prediction accuracy by cross-validation — fb_gblup_cv
text: Estimate accurately genomic BLUP predicts performance genotypes whose phenotypes held . fold variance components estimated REML training genotypes, held-breeding values predicted genomic relationship matrix (\(\hat{u}_V = K_{VT}(K_{TT} + \delta )^{-1}(y_T - X_T\hat\beta)\)), predictions compared observed phenotypes. headline metric prediction accuracy, correlation predicted observed across held-genotypes; bias slope observed predicted (1 unbiased).

title: Genomic-prediction accuracy by cross-validation — fb_gblup_cv
code: fb_gblup_cv(y, K, X = NULL, folds = 5L, repeats = 1L, seed = NULL, tol = 1e-08)

title: Genomic-prediction accuracy by cross-validation — fb_gblup_cv
text: y Numeric vector phenotypes, one per genotype, aligned rows K. K n x n genomic (pedigree) relationship matrix. X Optional n x p fixed-effect design matrix (intercept used NULL). folds Number cross-validation folds (default 5). repeats Number independent fold partitions average (default 1); repeated CV stabilises estimate. seed Optional integer seed reproducible fold assignment. tol PSD / symmetry tolerance spectral decomposition.

title: Genomic-prediction accuracy by cross-validation — fb_gblup_cv
text: fb_gblup_cv object: accuracy (pooled predicted-observed correlation), accuracy_per_fold, bias (pooled observed-- predicted slope), predicted (--fold predictions input order, averaged repeats), h2 (REML heritability full data), metadata.

title: Genomic-prediction accuracy by cross-validation — fb_gblup_cv
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n <- 80L; m <- 400L Z <- matrix(rbinom(n * m, 2L, 0.3), n, m) Zc <- scale(Z, scale = FALSE) G <- tcrossprod(Zc) / m + diag(n) * 1e-3 u <- as.vector(t(chol(G)) %*% rnorm(n)) y <- u + rnorm(n) cv <- fb_gblup_cv(y, G, folds = 5L, seed = 1L) cv$accuracy #> [1] -0.3181149 # }

what: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
text: Estimates location, scale, shape GEV distribution vector block-maxima observations maximum likelihood (base optim(), dependency-free deterministic given data).

title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
code: fb_gev(y, return_periods = c(10, 50, 100), conf_level = 0.95)

title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
text: y Numeric vector block-maxima observations. Must contain least four finite values. return_periods Numeric vector return periods (blocks) report return levels. Defaults c(10, 50, 100). conf_level Numeric (0, 1). interval level parameter summary. Defaults 0.95.

title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
text: object class c("fb_gev_fit", "fb_family_fit"): list estimates (data.frame term / estimate / std.error / conf.low / conf.high), return_levels (data.frame return_period / return_level), method, n_obs, logLik.

title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev
text: Maximum likelihood maximises GEV log-likelihood directly, scale optimised log scale keep positive standard errors recovered observed-information (Hessian) matrix delta method scale. Return levels (level exceeded per m blocks average) computed fitted parameters reported fit$return_levels requested return periods. scalable Bayesian GEV belongs INLA's native gev / bgev family planned future release; greta backend ships GEV distribution.

title: Fit a generalised extreme value distribution to block maxima — fb_gev

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the greta engine — fb_greta
code: fb_greta(...)

text: ... Arguments passed flexybayes() (e.g. fixed, random, rcov, data, family, prior, syntax), native greta_model / greta-source IR model-spec slot. backend argument pinned "greta"; conflicting backend value raises structured refusal. pre-v0.5.0 model =  native-graph argument remapped model-spec slot call-compatibility, fb_greta(model = m) still fits native graph.

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the greta engine — fb_greta
text: formula: object class "flexybayes" (greta fit). native greta_model: flexybayes_direct_greta object (subclass "flexybayes") carrying MCMC draws, GLM-compatible shim, canonical-name map; see flexybayes() shared structure.

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the greta engine — fb_greta
text: attach canonical-name map native graph (triangulate() can align INLA Stan fit), build intermediate representation first pass : fb_greta(fb_from_greta(model, canonical_names = c(...))).

title: Fit a flexyBayes model via the greta engine — fb_greta
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # live greta fit -- needs a working Python / TensorFlow stack data(sleepstudy, package = "lme4") fit <- fb_greta(Reaction ~ Days + (1 | Subject), data = sleepstudy,                 n_samples = 200, warmup = 200, chains = 1,                 verbose = FALSE, mcmc_verbose = FALSE) coef(fit) } # }

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
text: Test marker association phenotype correcting polygenic background population / family structure via genomic relationship matrix. fb_gwas() fits polygenic null mixed model estimate variance components REML, — holding components fixed (P3D approximation Kang et al. 2010) — tests every marker exact generalised least squares eigenbasis relationship matrix. single eigendecomposition shared spectral primitive; marker O(n) weighted-least-squares score test, whole-genome scan feasible without per-marker model fit.

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
code: fb_gwas(formula, data, markers, K = NULL, marker_map = NULL, tol = 1e-08)

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
text: formula two-sided formula y ~ covariates response fixed-effect covariates (intercept included unless removed - 1). Use y ~ 1 intercept-background. data data frame response covariates; one row per individual (association unit). markers numeric n x m matrix marker genotypes (allele dosages), one row per individual aligned data, one column per marker. Column names used marker identifiers. K n x n genomic (pedigree) relationship matrix. NULL (default) built centred markers \(G = Z_c Z_c^\top / m\), average-allele-frequency genomic relationship (Astle & Balding 2009). normalises marker count m, unlike VanRaden's (2008) first method, divides \(2\sum_j p_j(1 - p_j)\); two coincide every marker minor-allele frequency 0.5 otherwise differ constant scale REML variance ratio absorbs, scan statistics unaffected. Note building K markers tested causes proximal contamination: marker's signal enters polygenic term deflates test statistic (conservative, power loss). well-calibrated scan supply K independent background panel leave-one-chromosome-construction. marker_map Optional data frame columns marker, chr, pos annotate results order Manhattan plot. tol PSD / symmetry tolerance passed spectral primitive.

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
text: fb_gwas object: list results (data frame marker, effect, se, statistic (chi-square), p_value, p_bonferroni, q_value (Benjamini-Hochberg FDR), plus map columns), lambda_gc (genomic-control inflation factor), var_g / var_e / h2 (null REML components), metadata.

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
text: backends (greta / INLA / brms) used scan — deterministic frequentist fast path. enter handful significant loci re-fit full Bayesian models credible effect sizes, affordable reduced scale.

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas
text: Kang, H. M., et al. (2010). Variance component model account sample structure genome-wide association studies. Nature Genetics, 42(4), 348-354. Astle, W., & Balding, D. J. (2009). Population structure cryptic relatedness genetic association studies. Statistical Science, 24(4), 451-471. VanRaden, P. M. (2008). Efficient methods compute genomic predictions. Journal Dairy Science, 91(11), 4414-4423.

title: Genome-wide association scan (EMMAX / P3D) — fb_gwas

what: Emit a flexyBayes posterior as a log-density producer — fb_log_posterior
